questions: Why do you think some people are more willing to try new things than others?
Do you think it is important for young people to take risks and try new experiences? Why?
How has technology changed the range of new experiences available to people today?
Do you think society values people who take risks and try new things? Why or why not?
In what ways can trying new things contribute to a person's personal growth and development?
Who do people in your country most commonly admire — celebrities, politicians, or ordinary people?
Do you think young people today have good role models? Why or why not?
How important is it for young people to have someone to look up to?
Do you think social media has changed who people admire and look up to? How?
Should schools make a greater effort to teach children about historical figures they can admire?
How important do you think travel is for a person's personal development?
Do you think international travel has become too accessible? Are there any downsides to this?
How has tourism changed the way people understand and appreciate different cultures?
Do you think governments should do more to promote domestic tourism? Why?
What are the environmental consequences of modern travel, and what can be done to reduce them?
Why do you think some people feel very strongly attached to particular places?
How important are public spaces such as parks and squares in modern cities?
Do you think cities today provide enough spaces for people to relax and enjoy their surroundings?
How has urbanisation changed people's relationship with natural environments?
What responsibilities do governments have in preserving places that are important to local communities?
How important do you think it is for people to celebrate significant occasions together?
Do you think celebrations today have become too commercialised? Why do you think that is?
How have traditional celebrations changed in your country over recent generations?
Do you think national celebrations help to bring communities together? In what ways?
Are there any celebrations that you feel have lost their original meaning or purpose over time?

linked_topic: A Book or Film You Enjoyed
questions: Do you think people read fewer books today than in the past? What has caused this change?

How do you think digital media has changed people's reading and viewing habits?
Do you think it is important for children to read fiction as well as factual texts? Why?

linked_topic: A Skill You Would Like to Learn

How do gift-giving traditions vary between different cultures around the world?

Is it more meaningful to give someone an experience rather than a physical gift? Why?
How has online shopping changed the way people choose and buy gifts for others?
How important do you think childhood experiences are in shaping a person's character and values?
Do you think children today have a better childhood than children did a generation ago? Why?
How has technology changed the nature of childhood in your country?
Do you think parents today put too much pressure on their children to succeed?
Should children be given more freedom to play and explore independently, or do they benefit from more structure?
Why do you think some people are more willing to help others than others are?
How important is it for communities to take responsibility for looking after vulnerable members?
Do you think people in cities are generally less willing to help strangers than people in rural areas?
Should volunteering and helping others be made a compulsory part of school education? Why?
In what ways has technology affected people's willingness and ability to help those in need?

linked_topic: A Piece of Technology You Use
questions: Do you think people today have become too dependent on technology? In what ways?

How do you think artificial intelligence will change daily life over the next twenty years?

linked_topic: A Place You Would Like to Visit
questions: Why do you think people are so attracted to visiting new and unfamiliar places?

Do you think people today face more difficult decisions than previous generations did?
How does peer pressure influence the decisions that young people make about their lives?

linked_topic: A Sport or Physical Activity You Enjoy
questions: Why do you think participation in sport and physical activity is declining in many countries?

linked_topic: A Famous Person You Would Like to Meet

linked_topic: A Piece of Art or Music
